defaults.rule: Use fundamental-shapes.md as the shared visual grammar.
Allow restrained color only for page-mockup when the user explicitly wants webpage-like fidelity.
Do not leave empty placeholder boxes or decorative empty sections in the final diagram.
Do not repeat the same long text across multiple regions; if text wraps badly or duplicates, simplify or remove it.

diagram-rules.rule: A diagram shows structure. Notes show content. Never write sentences or long bullet lists inside boxes — that is notes, not a diagram.
Each shape label must be 1–5 words. If you need more, add another shape.
One concept per shape. If a topic has 10 concepts, use 10 shapes — not 1 shape with 10 bullets.
Max 3 short bullets per container, each 5 words or fewer. No paragraphs inside shapes.

delivery-modes.rule: Infer the mode from the first prompt. Pass it as --mode to open_diagram.py in step-03. Never render before the .excalidraw file is written and validated.

mode.trigger: default; show me; open; just a diagram; no explicit output format
mode.action: open_diagram.py --mode edit → writes open-edit.html (instant redirect to hosted Excalidraw editor) → opens in browser

mode.trigger: open animation; watch it animate; show animation; view animated
mode.action: open_diagram.py --mode animate → writes open-animate.html (instant redirect to hosted animation view) → opens in browser

mode.trigger: save the file; save as excalidraw; keep the source; download the diagram
mode.action: open_diagram.py --mode save-excalidraw → copies .excalidraw (and .animationinfo.json if present) to project directory

mode.trigger: save animation; animation video; animated SVG; save video
mode.action: open_diagram.py --mode save-animation → renders and saves .animated.svg to project directory (always workspace, never /tmp)

mode.trigger: save image; export PNG; save PNG; export image
mode.action: open_diagram.py --mode save-image → renders and saves .png to project directory

mode.trigger: show image; open image; open PNG; view image
mode.action: open_diagram.py --mode open-image → renders .png, saves to project directory, opens with system viewer

route.when: teach, explain simply, lesson, compare, ELI5
brainstorm, notes, cluster, opportunity map, organize ideas
journey, wireflow, sitemap, screen flow, onboarding, checkout
funnel, conversion, drop-off, lead stages, qualification
architecture, API, protocol, request flow, event flow, integration, retry
condition, symptom, diagnosis, treatment, patient explanation
full creative mode, raw creative mode, open composition, choose any diagram style, do whatever fits best
route.goal: use the best hand-drawn composition without forcing a narrow route

route.when: webpage, landing page, dashboard, pricing page, UI mockup
route.goal: read like a webpage with strong hierarchy

disambiguation.rule: If the page itself is the deliverable, use page-mockup. If the flow is the deliverable, use ux.
If the request is technical and educational, use technical-explainer first and borrow teaching simplicity only if needed.
If the request is exploratory, use ideation. If it is already structured, use the domain route instead.
Use creative-raw only when the user explicitly asks for broad creative freedom. Otherwise prefer a narrower route.
